info.title: gdshrapt
info.description:
GDShrapt.CLI is a .NET global tool for semantic analysis and safe refactoring of GDScript 2.0 (Godot 4.x) projects.

Key Features:
- Safe project-wide rename with confidence levels (Strict, Potential, NameMatch)
- Semantic validation with type inference (GD1xxx-GD8xxx diagnostics)
- Style linting with naming conventions and presets (GDLxxx rules)
- Auto-formatting with style extraction
- Dead code detection, dependency analysis, metrics, type coverage
- CI-friendly exit codes and JSON/SARIF output

Install: dotnet tool install -g GDShrapt.CLI
Usage: gdshrapt rename old_name new_name --diff

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription:
GDShrapt - Semantic analysis and safe refactoring for GDScript

Primary feature: safe project-wide rename with confidence levels.

Core commands:
  rename      Safe project-wide rename with confidence
  analyze     All diagnostics (lint + validate)
  check       CI-friendly exit codes
  validate    Syntax and semantic validation
  lint        Style and naming rules
  format      Auto-format GDScript files

Advanced commands:
  symbols, find-refs, type-coverage, dead-code, deps,
  metrics, stats, parse, extract-style, reorder-code,
  add-types, license, info

commands.name: rename
commands.description:
Rename a symbol across the project with confidence-aware safety levels.

Examples:
  gdshrapt rename speed new_speed              Rename by name
  gdshrapt rename speed new_speed --diff       Preview with diff
  gdshrapt rename speed new_speed --apply      Apply changes
  gdshrapt rename new_speed --file player.gd --line 5   Rename symbol at position

arguments.name: old-name
arguments.description: Current symbol name (or new name when using --line)

commands.name: analyze
commands.description:
Analyze a GDScript project and report all diagnostics (validation + linting).

Examples:
  gdshrapt analyze                         Analyze current directory
  gdshrapt analyze ./my-project            Analyze specific project
  gdshrapt analyze --format json           Output as JSON
  gdshrapt analyze --fail-on warning       Fail on warnings (for CI)

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Quick project health check with exit codes for CI/CD pipelines.

Examples:
  gdshrapt check                           Check current directory
  gdshrapt check --silent                  Only return exit code
  gdshrapt check --fail-on warning         Fail on warnings too

commands.name: validate
commands.description:
Validate GDScript for syntax errors, type mismatches, and semantic issues.

Examples:
  gdshrapt validate                        Validate current project
  gdshrapt validate --check-types          Only type checking
  gdshrapt validate --strict               Treat all issues as errors

commands.name: lint
commands.description:
Check GDScript files for style violations, naming conventions, and best practices.

Use --preset to apply predefined rule sets, or configure individual
options below. Individual options override preset values.
Most options can also be set in .gdshrapt.json (see 'gdshrapt init').

Examples:
  gdshrapt lint                            Lint current project
  gdshrapt lint --preset strict            Use strict rules
  gdshrapt lint --rules GDL001,GDL003      Run specific rules
  gdshrapt lint --category naming          Only naming rules

commands.name: format
commands.description: Format GDScript files

commands.name: symbols
commands.description:
List all symbols (classes, functions, variables, signals, constants) in a GDScript file.
Shows symbol kind, type annotation, and location.

Examples:
  gdshrapt symbols player.gd                             List all symbols
  gdshrapt symbols scripts/entity.gd --format json       Output as JSON
  gdshrapt symbols player.gd -p /path/to/project         Specify project path

commands.name: find-refs
commands.description:
Find all references to a symbol across the project.
Search by name or by position in a file (--line/--column).

Examples:
  gdshrapt find-refs health                              Find by symbol name
  gdshrapt find-refs take_damage -p ./my-project         Search in specific project
  gdshrapt find-refs health --file player.gd             Search in one file only
  gdshrapt find-refs --file player.gd --line 15          Find symbol at line 15
  gdshrapt find-refs health --explain                    Show confidence reasons

arguments.name: symbol
arguments.description: Symbol name to find references for (optional if --line is used)

commands.name: type-coverage
commands.description:
Report how much of your code has explicit type annotations.

Examples:
  gdshrapt type-coverage                   Project-wide coverage
  gdshrapt type-coverage --file player.gd  Single file coverage

commands.name: dead-code
commands.description:
Detect unused variables, functions, signals, and unreachable code.

Examples:
  gdshrapt dead-code                       Find all dead code
  gdshrapt dead-code --fail-if-found       Fail for CI if found
  gdshrapt dead-code --kind Function       Only unused functions
  gdshrapt dead-code --top 10              Show top 10 files
  gdshrapt dead-code --exclude-tests       Skip test files
  gdshrapt dead-code --explain             Show evidence details

commands.name: deps
commands.description:
Visualize file dependencies (extends, preload) and detect circular imports.

Examples:
  gdshrapt deps                            Show dependency graph
  gdshrapt deps --fail-on-cycles           Fail if cycles found
  gdshrapt deps --show-coupled             Show most coupled files

commands.name: metrics
commands.description:
Calculate cyclomatic complexity, maintainability index, and other metrics.

Examples:
  gdshrapt metrics                         All project metrics
  gdshrapt metrics --sort-by complexity     Sort by complexity
  gdshrapt metrics --top 10 --show-methods Top 10 with methods

commands.name: stats
commands.description:
Show a combined summary of project size, complexity, and health.

Examples:
  gdshrapt stats                           Project statistics
  gdshrapt stats --format json             JSON output

commands.name: parse
commands.description:
Parse a GDScript file and display its abstract syntax tree.

Examples:
  gdshrapt parse player.gd                 Show AST tree
  gdshrapt parse player.gd -o json         Output as JSON
  gdshrapt parse player.gd -o tokens       Show token stream

commands.name: extract-style
commands.description:
Detect formatting conventions from an existing GDScript file.

Examples:
  gdshrapt extract-style player.gd         Detect style (TOML)
  gdshrapt extract-style player.gd -o json Output as JSON

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: Path to the sample GDScript file

commands.name: reorder-code
commands.description: Reorder class members according to style guide

commands.name: add-types
commands.description: Add type annotations to variables

commands.name: license
commands.description: Manage Pro license

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show license status

commands.name: activate
commands.description: Activate a license

commands.name: info
commands.description: Show version and license information

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create a .gdshrapt.json configuration file.

Available presets: minimal, recommended, strict, relaxed, ci, local, team

Examples:
  gdshrapt init                             Create config in current directory
  gdshrapt init --preset strict             Create strict config
  gdshrapt init --preset ci                 CI-optimized config (fail-fast)
  gdshrapt init ./my-project --force        Overwrite existing config

commands.name: config
commands.description:
Manage .gdshrapt.json configuration.

Subcommands:
  init       Create a new configuration file
  show       Display current configuration
  validate   Check configuration for errors

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create a .gdshrapt.json configuration file with optional preset.

Available presets: minimal, recommended, strict, relaxed, ci, local, team

Examples:
  gdshrapt config init                          Create config with defaults
  gdshrapt config init --preset ci              CI-optimized config (fail-fast)
  gdshrapt config init --preset team            Team conventions (balanced)
  gdshrapt config init ./project --force        Overwrite existing config

commands.name: show
commands.description:
Display current project configuration.

By default shows the raw .gdshrapt.json content.
Use --effective to see resolved config (defaults + file).

Examples:
  gdshrapt config show                          Show raw config
  gdshrapt config show --effective              Show resolved config with defaults
  gdshrapt config show --format json            Output as JSON
  gdshrapt config show ./project --effective    Show effective config for project

commands.name: validate
commands.description:
Check .gdshrapt.json for errors (schema, ranges, conflicts).

Examples:
  gdshrapt config validate                      Validate current config
  gdshrapt config validate --explain            Show detailed explanations
  gdshrapt config validate ./project            Validate config in project dir

commands.name: duplicates
commands.description: [Experimental] Find duplicated code blocks (Pro)

commands.name: security
commands.description: [Experimental] Scan for security vulnerabilities (Pro)

commands.name: trends
commands.description: [Experimental] View metrics trends over time (Pro)
